metadata.name: Swampwalk — Bog Raiders attacks unblocked through defending Swamp
metadata.description: p1 controls Bog Raiders (2/2, Swampwalk). p2 controls a Swamp and a Hill Giant (3/3 vanilla creature). p1 attacks with Bog Raiders targeting p2. Because p2 controls a Swamp, Bog Raiders has swampwalk and cannot be blocked (CR 702.14c). p2 has a Hill Giant that would otherwise legally block a 2/2, but the swampwalk restriction (CR 509.1b) makes any declaration of Hill Giant as a blocker illegal. Bog Raiders is therefore an unblocked attacker and per CR 510.1b assigns its 2 damage to p2, reducing p2's life from 40 to 38. This tests the positive case: swampwalk is active when the defending player controls the matching land type. The negative case (no Swamp on p2's side means Hill Giant can block) is covered by unit tests.
metadata.generation_notes: CR 702.14b: Swampwalk is a landwalk ability, which is an evasion ability. CR 702.14c: A creature with swampwalk can't be blocked as long as the defending player controls at least one land with the subtype Swamp. p2 controls a Swamp (Basic Land — Swamp, confirmed by Scryfall oracle), which satisfies the condition. Hill Giant (3/3, Creature — Giant, no abilities) is controlled by p2 and would normally be a legal blocker for a 2/2 creature. However, CR 509.1b requires the defending player to check whether each chosen blocker obeys all restrictions. Because Bog Raiders has swampwalk and p2 controls a Swamp, any declaration of Hill Giant as a blocker for Bog Raiders violates the restriction — the declaration would be illegal (CR 509.1b). Bog Raiders is therefore an unblocked attacker. CR 510.1b: An unblocked creature assigns its combat damage to the player, planeswalker, or battle it's attacking. Bog Raiders (power 2) assigns 2 damage to p2, reducing p2's life from 40 to 38. Card definitions used: Bog Raiders ({2}{B}, Creature — Zombie 2/2, keyword Landwalk(BasicType(Swamp))) confirmed in definitions.rs line 1462. Swamp (Basic Land — Swamp, {T}: Add {B}) confirmed in definitions.rs line 474. Hill Giant is not in definitions.rs but is used only for the battlefield and is a recognized Scryfall oracle card (3/3, Creature — Giant, no abilities). All combat steps are documentary (turn_based_action) — the harness does not execute combat actions, consistent with scripts 004-009. The final assert_state checks initial life totals only (the life-total change is documented in the sba_check note as the expected CR outcome). A dispute is filed noting the harness cannot assert the life change after combat.

disputes.description: Harness does not execute combat from turn_based_action steps — the unblocked damage dealing (Bog Raiders 2 damage to p2, reducing life from 40 to 38) cannot be asserted after the script runs. The CR reasoning is unambiguous: swampwalk triggers CR 702.14c when p2 controls a Swamp, blocking restriction propagates via CR 509.1b, and unblocked damage goes to the defending player via CR 510.1b. The expected final state (p2 life 38) is documented in generation_notes and the combat damage step note. This is consistent with the approach taken in scripts 004-009 which also use documentary turn_based_action steps without executable life-change assertions.

script.step_note: Bog Raiders (2/2, Swampwalk) attacks p2; Hill Giant cannot legally block because p2 controls a Swamp.

actions.description: Initial: Bog Raiders (2/2 Swampwalk) on p1 battlefield; Swamp and Hill Giant on p2 battlefield; life totals 40/40
actions.note: p2 controls a Swamp — swampwalk condition (CR 702.14c) is already satisfied before combat begins.

actions.description: Declare attackers: p1 attacks with Bog Raiders targeting p2
actions.note: CR 508.1: p1 declares Bog Raiders as attacker targeting p2. Bog Raiders becomes tapped (CR 508.1f). Bog Raiders has swampwalk (CR 702.14b: a landwalk ability, which is an evasion ability).

actions.description: Declare blockers step: Hill Giant cannot legally block Bog Raiders — swampwalk restriction is active because p2 controls a Swamp
actions.note: CR 509.1b: The defending player checks each chosen blocker against restrictions. CR 702.14c: Bog Raiders has swampwalk; p2 controls at least one land with the subtype Swamp (a Swamp). Therefore Bog Raiders cannot be blocked — any attempt to declare Hill Giant as a blocker would be illegal. p2 declares no blockers. Bog Raiders is an unblocked attacker (CR 509.1h).

actions.description: Combat damage assignment: Bog Raiders (unblocked, power 2) assigns 2 damage to p2
actions.note: CR 510.1b: An unblocked creature assigns its combat damage to the player it's attacking. Bog Raiders (power 2) assigns all 2 damage to p2. p2 life total: 40 - 2 = 38.
CR 704.5a: p2 now has 38 life (above 0), so no SBA triggers for life total. No creatures received lethal damage. No SBAs apply.

actions.description: Post-combat state as visible to harness: life totals unchanged (combat damage not executed by harness); Bog Raiders still on p1 battlefield
actions.note: Harness does not execute combat — life totals remain 40/40 in the harness state. The expected CR outcome (p2 life 38, Bog Raiders tapped on p1 battlefield) is documented in the turn_based_action steps above and in generation_notes. See dispute entry for explanation.
